transport.bind_policy: localhost or explicit IPv4 loopback only
transport.health: GET /healthz is anonymous and returns service/version only
transport.readiness: api serve prints one JSON line with the actual bound port and token_file; --port 0 is supported

authentication.file_policy: regular non-symlink file, mode 0600, bounded size, minimum token length 32
authentication.browser_policy: the Console reads the token server-side; it must never enter browser JavaScript, URLs, logs, screenshots, or Git

request_rules.mutations: agent and canonical project attribution are required
request_rules.port_release: lease_id or port plus acting agent and owning project
request_rules.server_launch: structured argv is preferred; legacy cmd is parsed without a shell and rejects shell operators
request_rules.exact_lease_start: lease_id requires structured argv and an active unbound manual lease owned by the same agent/project
request_rules.docker: real commands resolve an absolute Docker entry point and are bounded by observation/lifecycle timeouts
request_rules.durable_lifecycle: target_kind is project, server, container, or worktree; repository is accepted only as compatibility input and is normalized to project; archive/purge always require a fresh plan and apply is bound to plan_id plus plan_fingerprint; restore requires explicit=true

event_contract.observe: POST /v1/observe accepts exactly {agent,project} and commits one explicit full-Docker host observation; repeated unchanged samples append no transition events and unavailable or unobservable capabilities never invent stopped resources
event_contract.read: GET /v1/events returns schema_version, events, next_cursor, and has_more in durable insertion-sequence order; after is the opaque high-watermark cursor from the prior page and limit is 1 through 500. occurred_at is event information, not the pagination key, so a slow operation committed later cannot fall behind the cursor merely because its event time is earlier
event_contract.privacy: diagnostic_json and other private operation evidence are never returned by the event feed
event_contract.catch_up: persist next_cursor only after the consumer durably handles every returned event; when has_more is true, request the next page immediately before polling again

state.read_model: GET /v1/state is a compatibility control snapshot derived from normalized SQLite; it is not the authoritative persistence format
state.persistence: server-wide service-owned 0700 authority directory and 0600 coordinator.sqlite3; authenticated clients use the Unix broker and retain only private execution journals
state.durable_ports: successful server start/register pins project+name to a port until explicit unassign or attributed state reset
state.operations: short reservation/commit lock phases with same-target exclusion and bounded journal evidence

inventory_contract.ordinary_inventory: GET /v1/inventory returns the single server-wide broker snapshot without runtime sampling or persistence, including servers published by other enrolled UIDs
inventory_contract.compatibility_policy: legacy Console rows are nested under v1_compatibility; non-colliding legacy aliases may also appear at top level, but top-level leases and port_assignments always retain normalized lease_id/assignment_id and repo_id identities
inventory_contract.no_docker: GET /v1/inventory/no-docker returns the authenticated committed normalized snapshot without a Docker CLI/daemon probe; when the systemd readiness client supplies exactly one project, name, and port query target, only compatibility rows that can affect that registration graph receive bounded current process, listener, and health observation on an in-memory copy; the route excludes unrelated services, process-usage sampling, and backup discovery and never persists derived lifecycle or lease changes
inventory_contract.project_usage: rows include stable usage_key plus authoritative server_ids and exact container_resource_ids; container_names remain legacy display data and are not ownership keys
inventory_contract.docker_ownership: Compose labels and verified coordinator sidecars are mutation authority; name similarity is read-only evidence
inventory_contract.project_reports: ok=false must be treated as failure even when HTTP status is 200; inspect partial, preflight_failed, classification, actions, and action_errors

lifecycle_contract.archives: GET /v1/archives returns {archives:[...]}; rows carry target_kind, target_id, display_name, archived_at, reason, actor and may include project_id/parent, restorable, removable, status, retained, effects, and blockers
lifecycle_contract.plan: POST /v1/lifecycle/plan accepts {target_kind,target_id,action:'archive'|'purge',reason,agent}; the response binds plan_id and plan_fingerprint (fingerprint is a compatibility alias) to target/effects/retained/deleted/blockers and supplies the exact purge confirmation_phrase
lifecycle_contract.apply: POST /v1/lifecycle/apply accepts exactly {plan_id,plan_fingerprint,confirmation_phrase}; confirmation_phrase is the empty string for archive and the exact server-issued phrase for purge
lifecycle_contract.restore: POST /v1/lifecycle/restore accepts {target_kind,target_id,reason,agent,explicit:true}; restore clears the durable fence and never implies that the resource started
lifecycle_contract.result_truth: apply and restore return ok, status, partial, needs_attention, and errors; ok=false, partial=true, needs_attention=true, or blocked/failed/partial status is failure even with HTTP 200
lifecycle_contract.retention: archive stops and fences the exact resource while retaining declared data/history; permanent removal is available only for an archived row that advertises removable=true; worktree removal is shown only as an advertised removable child of an archived project

errors.400: invalid request, ownership mismatch, capability failure, or refused mutation
errors.409: fresh lifecycle evidence blocks the action, the archived capability is unavailable, or an apply/restore result is incomplete
errors.415: POST content type is not application/json
